campbell.title: Campbell City Council — May 19, 2026
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council held a meeting on May 19, 2026 where they discussed staffing vacancies and the city's efforts to recruit and retain employees. The council also addressed public concerns about property security and announced that the Boogie Festival is happening this weekend.
keyTopics: Staffing vacancies and recruitment/retention efforts
Property security concerns
Boogie Festival this weekend

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — May 20, 2026
saratoga.summary: The City Council adopted a resolution to identify certain properties in Saratoga and scheduled a public hearing for June 17, 2026, where residents can voice concerns before the council makes a final decision.
keyTopics: Resolution adopted to identify designated properties
Public hearing scheduled for June 17, 2026
Residents can submit protests and comments before final approval

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos Town Council — June 2, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos Town Council held a meeting on June 2, 2026, to approve meeting minutes, confirm a new Youth Commissioner, and set up the November 2026 municipal election. The council also approved final plans for a new housing development, authorized multi-year contracts with software and service vendors, and approved the budget.
keyTopics: November 3, 2026 municipal election scheduled
New Youth Commissioner appointed by selection committee
Final approval for Tract 10675 Boulevard development project
Multi-year software and services contracts authorized

san-jose.title: San José City Council — June 10, 2026
san-jose.summary: The San José City Council held a meeting to organize and finalize upcoming agendas for June 16 and June 23, 2026. The council reviewed which items to add to each agenda, set specific times for certain discussions, and checked whether any agenda items needed special notice or waivers. The Community and Economic Development Committee's work plan was also discussed.
keyTopics: Finalized the June 16, 2026 meeting agenda with new items and timing assignments
Reviewed the draft June 23, 2026 agenda and determined additions needed
Community and Economic Development Committee work plan discussion

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — June 9, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council met to appoint a new City Attorney during a private closed session, then held a public meeting to officially recognize Pride Month and Juneteenth with proclamations celebrating both observances.
keyTopics: City Attorney appointment (discussed in closed session)
Pride Month proclamation
Juneteenth proclamation

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — June 2, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Sunnyvale City Council held two back-to-back special meetings on June 2, 2026. The first was a closed session (private meeting) where sensitive matters were discussed, followed by a public special meeting with items on a special order of the day agenda.
keyTopics: Closed session meeting held at 5:30 PM
Special order of the day items discussed at 6:30 PM public meeting

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — June 15, 2026
cupertino.summary: The Cupertino City Council held a closed-door meeting with legal counsel to discuss an anticipated lawsuit the city may initiate. The council also reported back to the public on what was discussed in that closed session and reminded residents and organizations about the city's lobbying registration requirements.
keyTopics: City considering initiating a new lawsuit (1 case in progress)
Closed session legal strategy discussion with city attorneys
Lobbying registration and reporting requirements under city ordinance

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — June 1, 2026
santa-clara.summary: Santa Clara's City Council held a Stadium Neighborhood Relations Ad Hoc Subcommittee meeting on June 1, 2026. Residents could participate in person or remotely via Zoom, with options to submit written comments ahead of time.
keyTopics: Stadium neighborhood relations discussion
In-person and remote public participation options available
Written public comments accepted via email before meetings

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — April 7, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council met to approve routine administrative items and discuss updates to the city's federal housing and community development funding plan. The council also reviewed requests from individual members for new work items and considered changes to how council vacancies are filled.
keyTopics: Amendment to federal housing/community development funding plan (2025-2030)
Changes to council vacancy procedures
New work requests from individual councilmembers
Approval of previous meeting minutes and upcoming meeting calendar

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — April 28, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The Palo Alto City Council held a meeting covering updates from city departments and committees, discussed improvements to the Foothills Nature Preserve, and addressed the future management and direction of the middle school athletics program. The council also reviewed the Parks and Recreation Commission's work plan for the upcoming fiscal year.
keyTopics: Foothills Nature Preserve Improvements Project – planned enhancements to the preserve
Middle School Athletics Program – discussion on how the program will be managed and what direction it will take
Parks and Recreation Commission Work Plan for 2027 – review and confirmation of planned activities
Committee and liaison updates from various city boards and commissions
